channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sadio Mané: Senegal’s Veteran Leader Heads into 2026 World Cup
item.description: Sadio Mané, now 34, continues to be the standout figure of Senegalese football as the country heads into the 2026 World Cup in North America. Having been inspired by Senegal's remarkable 2002 World Cu...

item.title: Rishabh Pant Faces Setbacks in International Cricket and IPL
item.description: Rishabh Pant, the Indian wicketkeeper-batter and captain of Lucknow Super Giants, has experienced a difficult period in his cricketing career. Recently relieved of his vice-captaincy in Tests, he has ...

item.title: Rare 1933 Harley-Davidson Motorcycles Photographed on Milwaukee’s Bradford Beach
item.description: In 1933, Harley-Davidson introduced a distinctive, one-year-only paint job for its motorcycles, now considered one of the most striking designs to emerge from the Milwaukee-based manufacturer. To show...

item.title: Saad Edhi safely returns to Istanbul after Gaza flotilla detention
item.description: Pakistan confirmed on Thursday that humanitarian worker Saad Edhi, grandson of the late philanthropist Abdul Sattar Edhi, has been released after being detained by Israeli forces during the intercepti...

item.title: Victor Thompson Accused Of Using Fake Mining Licence To Defraud American Investors
item.description: For one serious case wey dey Federal High Court, Uyo, Victor Ekpong Thompson don dey accused say e use fake mining licence take con US investors. Michael Eyo, wey be second prosecution witness, talk s...

item.title: Zayn Malik faces challenges during comeback after health-related tour cancellations
item.description: Zayn Malik's highly anticipated comeback encountered difficulties on Thursday when water bottles were thrown at him as he exited a fan Q&A in London at Banquet Records. The 33-year-old singer had alre...

item.title: US and Iran Show Limited Progress in Talks Amid Ceasefire
item.description: The United States and Iran remain in a tense standoff over Tehran’s uranium stockpile and control of the Strait of Hormuz, key points in the ongoing conflict. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio noted s...

item.title: Two-Time NASCAR Champion Kyle Busch Passes Away at 41
item.description: Kyle Busch, the two-time NASCAR Cup Series champion, has tragically passed away at the age of 41, as confirmed by NASCAR officials. Busch was widely regarded as one of the most skilled and competitive...

item.title: Roger Goodell Clarifies Seahawks Ownership Market Has Strong Interest
item.description: NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell addressed recent reports suggesting a 'soft' market for the sale of the Seattle Seahawks. According to an unnamed team owner, there were claims that interest in the team...

item.title: 2027 Ram 1500 Rumble Bee: New High-Performance Street Truck Range
item.description: Ram is launching a new range of high-performance street trucks called the 2027 Ram 1500 Rumble Bee, aiming to revive the concept of muscle trucks for everyday use. The lineup includes the entry-level ...

item.title: SpaceX delays Starship test flight due to technical difficulties at Texas launch site
item.description: SpaceX came very close to launching its largest Starship rocket yet on Thursday evening, only to be halted by a series of last-minute technical issues. The 124-metre (407-foot) vehicle was scheduled f...

item.title: Alberta’s Proposed Referendum Raises Uncertainty Over Separatism Debate
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has introduced a new question for the province's October referendum, intended to address the ongoing debate over separatism. The proposed question is not a straightforwa...

item.title: The Life, Crimes, and Execution of Peter Kürten, the ‘Vampire of Düsseldorf’
item.description: Peter Kürten, born in 1883 in Cologne, became one of Germany's most infamous serial killers, earning the nickname 'Vampire of Düsseldorf' due to his admitted habit of drinking his victims' blood. Comi...

item.title: Auditor-General report highlights failures in Australia's FOI system
item.description: A recent report by the Australian auditor-general has revealed systemic issues in the country's Freedom of Information (FOI) system. According to the report, the FOI framework consistently fails to pr...

item.title: Pakistan Mediates Between US and Iran Amid Tensions Over Hormuz
item.description: Islamabad is actively working to mediate between the United States and Iran to prevent further escalation in the ongoing conflict. Pakistan’s Interior Minister, Mohsin Naqvi, visited Tehran twice in a...

item.title: Global Health Authorities Explore Vaccines and Treatments for Bundibugyo Ebola Outbreak
item.description: Health authorities worldwide are urgently working to develop medical responses to the Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, caused by the Bundibugyo strain. This strain differs from ...

item.title: Torre expresses willingness to assist in search for Dela Rosa
item.description: Former official Torre has expressed his readiness to participate in the ongoing search for Dela Rosa, emphasizing his commitment to public service and civic responsibility. This statement comes amid h...

item.title: Etta James: Iconic Stage Performances of the 1970s
item.description: In the 1970s, Etta James showcased a remarkable blend of vulnerability and raw power in her live performances. Having overcome personal struggles, including addiction and hospitalizations, she emerged...

item.title: U.S. suspends joint defence board due to Canada’s delays in military spending plans
item.description: The U.S. government has decided to suspend the Permanent Joint Board on Defence with Canada, citing Ottawa’s lack of a detailed plan to increase military spending to 5 per cent of GDP by 2035 and ongo...

item.title: Iran conflict causes shortage of bitumen, slowing highway construction in India
item.description: The Indian government is facing a serious shortage of bitumen, largely due to the ongoing conflict in West Asia. This shortage is expected to significantly affect the construction and maintenance of t...
